generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time occupancy data for space utilization analytics and energy optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Measures environmental temperature for HVAC control and thermal comfort analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks energy consumption metrics for performance benchmarking and compliance verification"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralIntegritySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors structural health parameters for predictive maintenance and safety compliance"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects ambient conditions (humidity, air quality) for operational optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralIntegritySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BimGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational static model integrating geometric, spatial, and semantic data via IFC standards"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BimSemanticModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic layer enabling lifecycle-aware simulations through enriched BIM data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler
]

instance BuildingControlSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model for BCS components including sensors, actuators, and controllers"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimIntegrationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Dynamic data including sensor readings, actuator states, and environmental metrics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, StructuralIntegrityDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo IoTDataIngestionEnabler, DigitalTwinFrameworkEnabler
]

instance StaticMetadataData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Device configurations, control logic, and static BIM attributes"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinFrameworkEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Building Information Modeling framework serving as single source of truth for physical asset representation"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, CollaborativeLifecycleManagementService
]

instance IoTDataIngestionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono lightweight M2M service for secure real-time sensor data collection from heterogeneous devices"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ControlAndAutomationService
]

instance DigitalTwinFrameworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto digital twin framework providing device abstraction and state management for dynamic asset representations"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService, DecisionSupportVisualizationService, ControlAndAutomationService
]

instance HighFidelityVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine for high-fidelity 3D/4D visualization supporting immersive analytics with real-time data overlays"
    DTDFVocab:enables DecisionSupportVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data ingestion and validation from heterogeneous IoT sensors and BIM-integrated models to track operational states"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight, AnomalyDetectionInsight, ComplianceVerificationInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven simulations and what-if analyses to forecast degradation, failures, or efficiency losses"
    DTDFVocab:provides DegradationForecastInsight, FailurePredictionInsight, EfficiencyOptimizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ProactiveMaintenanceAction, CostBenefitOptimizationAction
]

instance DecisionSupportVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/4D visualizations presenting spatial-temporal insights with real-time data overlays"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpatialTemporalInsight, OccupancyPatternInsight, EnergyHotspotInsight
]

instance ControlAndAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop integration with physical systems enabling automated adjustments based on DT-derived insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveControlAction, RemoteCommissioningAction
]

instance CollaborativeLifecycleManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Cross-domain data exchange ensuring consistency across design, construction, and operations phases"
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignToOperationHandoverInsight, RetrofitPlanningInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of equipment performance, energy consumption, and environmental conditions"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

instance ComplianceVerificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification against energy codes and safety regulations"
]

instance DegradationForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted timeline for component degradation based on operational data"
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted failure points in building systems"
]

instance EfficiencyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified opportunities for energy and operational efficiency improvements"
]

instance SpatialTemporalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized patterns of space utilization and temporal usage variations"
]

instance OccupancyPatternInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analyzed occupancy trends for space optimization"
]

instance EnergyHotspotInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified areas with high energy consumption requiring attention"
]

instance ProactiveMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated scheduling of maintenance activities based on predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CostBenefitOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for refurbishment timing and investment prioritization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of lighting, HVAC, and other systems based on real-time conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance RemoteCommissioningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote configuration and calibration of building automation systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DesignToOperationHandoverInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Consolidated information for seamless transition from design to operational phases"
]

instance RetrofitPlanningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Data-driven recommendations for building system upgrades and renovations"
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system enables **control flows from the digital to physical domain** through **Facility Managers**, who interact with the deployed DT for **real-time monitoring and control**. The framework supports **bidirectional communication**, allowing the DT to influence physical building operations via validated commands. While the document does not specify exact **command types** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting control) or **control message formats**, the **modular architecture** and **runtime configuration management** suggest adaptable control logic. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied by the non-functional requirements (NF_X), which prioritize **interoperability** and **accessibility**, though no explicit security protocols (e.g., RBAC, digital signatures) are mentioned. **Feedback systems** are supported via the **central hub’s bidirectional data exchange**, enabling the DT to receive operational responses from physical devices. **Conflict resolution** and **command execution confirmation** are not explicitly detailed, but the framework’s **low-overhead updates** and **scalability** imply mechanisms to handle dynamic adjustments without redeployment."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling **immersive interfaces for monitoring and control**. The framework supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, which implies adaptable synchronization frequencies tailored to operational needs. While specific latency values or granularity thresholds (e.g., sub-second or millisecond-level) are not explicitly defined, the emphasis on **real-time** implies low-latency bidirectional communication to maintain operational fidelity. The system is designed to bridge static BIM representations with dynamic AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) requirements, ensuring temporal alignment across the building lifecycle."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **coordinated, role-based orchestration pattern** where the **DT Framework** acts as a central hub integrating **enriched BIM models** into operational Digital Twins. The architecture leverages **modular components** with **standardized extension points** for service integration, enabling a **plug-and-play** approach to add custom analytics or control services. Resource management is implied through **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments to system behavior without full redeployment. System-wide coordination is achieved via **bidirectional data exchange** between physical assets (e.g., sensors, actuators) and digital representations, with **Building Control Engineers**, **Facility Managers**, and **DT Engineers** contributing to distinct phases of the DT lifecycle (deployment, monitoring, extension). The framework emphasizes **holistic building lifecycle management**, though no explicit orchestration protocols (e.g., event-driven, microservices) are specified."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, encompassing the following phases:
  1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within the **BIM (Building Information Modeling) environment**, addressing BIM’s limitation in representing Building Control Systems (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Enrichment and Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models. This phase involves **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention, ensuring seamless transition from static BIM representations to dynamic DT models.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling real-time synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase includes **runtime synchronization** to maintain continuous alignment between the physical and virtual systems.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to monitor system states and operate physical infrastructure. This phase emphasizes **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control, supported by real-time data exchange capabilities.
  5. **Custom Service Integration and Evolution**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via a **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. This phase allows the DT to evolve dynamically with changing operational requirements, ensuring adaptability throughout its lifecycle.
  **Lifecycle Governance**: The framework supports the **complete life cycle of DT deployment and operation**, addressing gaps between BIM’s static representations and the DT’s dynamic nature. Governance is implicit in the structured use cases (e.g., device modeling, service development) and the modular, extensible architecture, which ensures maintainability and scalability."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework evaluates the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** to quantify the alignment between the physical building and its digital twin, ensuring fidelity in real-time synchronization. While explicit validation methods (e.g., formal verification, uncertainty quantification) are not detailed, the system relies on **automated model transformations** from BIM to DT artifacts to mitigate discrepancies. **Quality assurance** is implicitly supported through the **real-time data exchange** mechanism, which synchronizes physical states (e.g., sensor readings, actuator commands) with the digital twin. However, the document does not specify **error detection/correction mechanisms**, **uncertainty sources**, or **precision metrics** for individual models or data streams. The ODTE metric serves as a high-level evaluative criterion for assessing the twin’s operational coherence."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system relies on a **central hub architecture** to facilitate **bidirectional data exchange** between physical and digital components. While the document does not specify **communication protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT), **network topology**, or **bandwidth/latency requirements**, the framework’s **modular design** and **standardized extension points** enable integration with heterogeneous systems. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are implied by the non-functional requirements (NF_X), which emphasize **scalability** and **maintainability**, though no explicit redundancy protocols (e.g., failover, load balancing) are described. **Security protocols** for network communication are not mentioned, nor are details like **TLS/SSL encryption** or **authentication frameworks**. The framework’s adaptability is ensured through **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments without full redeployment, but no specifics on **network architecture** (e.g., edge/cloud hybrid) or **latency thresholds** are provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system is deployed using a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling integration of custom services and analytics. While the document does not specify **hosting platforms** (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise), the non-functional requirements (NF_X) highlight **scalability** and **maintainability**, suggesting support for **scalable deployment models**. Computational and storage requirements are not detailed, but the framework’s **runtime configuration management** implies **low-overhead updates** and **dynamic resource allocation**. The **DT Framework** acts as a central hub, facilitating **holistic building life cycle management**, though no explicit **deployment models** (e.g., containerized, serverless) or **availability/reliability assurances** (e.g., SLAs) are mentioned. The system’s adaptability is further supported by its ability to evolve with operational needs without full redeployment, but no specifics on **infrastructure technologies** (e.g., Kubernetes, NoSQL) are provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally with **external systems** through a **modular architecture** designed for **interoperability** and **scalability**. While the document explicitly mentions the use of **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** for **real-time data exchange**, it does not detail specific **data exchange protocols** (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) or **interoperability standards** (e.g., IFC, CityGML) beyond the implied BIM integration. The framework leverages **Unreal Engine** for **rich, interactive visualization**, suggesting dependencies on **graphics rendering pipelines** for immersive interfaces. The system’s **extension points** support integration with **enterprise systems** or other Digital Twins, but no explicit dependencies on third-party APIs or enterprise protocols (e.g., SAP, Oracle) are described. The integration pattern prioritizes **bidirectional data flow** and **heterogeneous system compatibility**, aligning with the AECO domain’s need for seamless collaboration between static BIM models and dynamic operational data."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **Building Information Modeling (BIM) as the foundational standard** for DT deployment, addressing BIM’s limitations by introducing a **model-driven methodology** to standardize semantic representations of Building Control System (BCS) components (sensors, actuators, controllers). The architecture incorporates **standardized extension points** to ensure modularity and interoperability, enabling seamless integration of custom services and third-party plugins. While the document does not explicitly mention **specific industry standards** (e.g., IFC, COBie), it emphasizes the use of **BIM as the de facto standard in the construction industry** and a **modular, service-oriented approach** to DT development. Compliance is implicitly ensured through the framework’s adherence to **MDE principles** and the structured transformation of BIM models into deployable DT artifacts. **Interoperability** is supported by the modular design, allowing for **plug-and-play integration** of services while maintaining operational consistency."
]
